feature/issue-58-integrate-coderlm-adapt.task_description: Add coderlm status indicator to nf-statusline.js: read coderlm.pid and render ● coderlm (green if running, PID alive) or nothing (not installed/stopped) after the River indicator. Write coderlm.state.json on start/stop in coderlm-lifecycle.cjs as a secondary signal. Fail-open: any read error renders nothing.
feature/issue-58-integrate-coderlm-adapt.approach: Add a coderlm status indicator (● coderlm) to nf-statusline.js that reads coderlm.pid file to determine if the coderlm server is running (green if alive, nothing if stopped/not installed), render it after the River indicator, and write coderlm.state.json in coderlm-lifecycle.cjs on start/stop as a secondary state signal.
out_of_scope: Changing the coderlm binary download, spawn, or health check logic in coderlm-lifecycle.cjs
Adding user configuration options for the coderlm indicator display
Implementing process monitoring beyond simple PID file existence and signal 0 checks
Integrating coderlm status into other hooks or workflow files outside nf-statusline.js
feature/issue-58-integrate-coderlm-adapt.risk_reason: Hook file modification (nf-statusline.js) is explicitly disqualified from low-risk classification per guardrails; requires dual-copy sync to hooks/dist/ and installer invocation.
